blocks.content: Chapter 181: Reversal of the Law (1)

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Fine, I'll explain. This place is called Purgatory, also known as the Prison of the Earth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Purgatory? Here?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Yes, it's a prison on earth. It's where heretics who reject the laws live. Now it makes sense. You must have denied the existence of God in the land of the earth. That's why you ended up here.
Shirone did not correct her misunderstanding.
Explaining it wouldn't make her understand, and it wasn't entirely wrong. None of them believed in Aker anyway.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: You must be descendants of the Nor, given that you can use magic. Unfortunately, there's nothing left for you here in Purgatory except death. Eventually, you'll all die.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, we will eventually die. But so will you.
Kanya lifted her chin, looking a bit proud.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Absolutely not. I am one of the chosen ones by God. I am guaranteed eternal life.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone recalled the myths of Kergos. If someone could grant eternal life, they would surely be considered a god.
But it didn't seem realistic. All living things aged and eventually died. Not even magic could prevent death.
Now that Kanya understood who they were, she seemed more at ease.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: It can't be helped. You're lucky. Follow me, I'll show you a place where you can stay.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why are you being so nice all of a sudden? If you're a divine subject and we're heretics, shouldn't you be treating us badly?
Kanya turned around, knowing no one would follow her.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: You're very suspicious, which proves you are descendants of the Nor. In Heaven, I would have driven you out, but here it's fine. In Purgatory, no law can protect divine subjects. We're in the same situation. Anyway, we need to hurry. It's dangerous after sunset.
As they ventured deeper into the canyon, the cliffs grew taller.
It was a desolate place with not even a single flying insect.
There was some moisture and echoing sounds.
In the vastness of nature, only their footsteps could be heard.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We won't encounter anything strange, will we?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Don't worry. The Valley of the Vortex Serpent is governed by a powerful law, so dangerous monsters like the Dokgakgui don't live here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What kind of law?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: The Vortex Serpent. It's a monster said to have lived here for over ten thousand years. But it should be sleeping now. It doesn't come out during the day. That's why we need to hurry. We must reach the Nor's Haven before sunset.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You said we are descendants of the Nor. Then, aren't the Nor divine subjects?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Of course, they are.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then why is there a Nor's Haven in Purgatory?
Kanya felt frustrated but remembered where they had come from and added an explanation.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: There are three races in Heaven. The Nor are one of them. Divine subjects have a predetermined lifespan from birth, and when it ends, they gain eternal life. But it seems some Nor couldn't accept this. So, they escaped to Purgatory and created an independent society.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone didn't fully understand the concept of a predetermined lifespan, but he let it go for now. At least it was less strange than the idea of gaining eternal life.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you, Kanya. You're very kind.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: What are you talking about? No divine subject is kind to heretics. We're going there because we need something too, so don't misunderstand. Once we reach the Nor's Haven, don't expect any acknowledgment from me.
Despite her harsh words, she couldn't meet Shirone's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: That's right. My sister is going because she needs something. But I'm going because I genuinely want to help.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! Thanks, Rena. You're very kind.
Rena blushed at Shirone's compliment.
Kanya stopped walking.
She understood that it was natural for her sister to be drawn to the stranger during this sensitive time, but as a divine subject under the law, such thoughts were unacceptable.
She grabbed Rena's wrist and pulled her towards the cliff.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Rena, what's wrong with you? You know we can never be with heretics. Even having such thoughts is blasphemy.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: I know. But that guy is…
Kanya looked at Rena in disbelief.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No means no! You may be naïve, but it's not all about looks. Choosing a man like that can lead to disaster.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: How would you know? You don't have a boyfriend either.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: You'll understand when you grow up. Anyway, listen to me, okay? From now on, be cold to that guy.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: It's not because he's handsome, it's because he's kind.
Kanya looked at Rena in disbelief.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Kind? That guy?
Shirone was certainly a unique boy but she felt that his free-spiritedness was because he was a heretic. Such freedom led to corruption.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Tell me clearly. Do you like handsome guys or kind guys?

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: Of course, both. He has to be handsome and kind.
Kanya looked at Rena in frustration.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Ugh, I really can't understand you.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: Don't worry, sister. I won't fall in love with someone I've only known for a day. And... we're not so different from them right now.
Divine subjects entering Purgatory without permission was a violation of the law. They could be punished, but it was something they couldn't give up.
However, bringing her sister along was something she regretted.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Even if Rena had a longer lifespan than she did, it was an irresponsible decision as her sister.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Rena. I…

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: I know, sister. Besides, I insisted on coming. You don't have to take on all the risks alone. I want to give our mother a gift too.
Kanya patted Rena's head.
The child who once cried for milk had grown up so much.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: She hoped Rena would receive the divine grace and enjoy eternal life. Because she was a lovely child, Ra would surely grant it.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Alright, let's go quickly.
The canyon was turning into a maze.
Shallow streams flowed through narrow paths, making the air damp.
Amy noticed, using her Red Eye, that Kanya's route was becoming erratic.
It was typical behavior for someone who was lost.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's quite far. Is the Nor's Haven still a long way off?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: We're not going there. We don't know where the Nor's Haven is.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What? You said we were going to the Nor's Haven.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: That's our destination. There's no way we, divine subjects, would know where the place made by the escaped Nor is.
Sensing Amy's frustration, Kanya added.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Don't worry. We still have a way.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What way?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: We'll use the drone. The Nor's Haven is an open market where anyone can go. A guide from the market is waiting in the Valley of the Vortex Serpent. We just need to find that person. So, the drone is searching for a place where someone could stay.
Shirone looked up at the two drones flying in the sky.
Those flying machines would be considered ancient relics in his world. However, their technology was far more advanced.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He wondered what expression Nade would make if he saw them.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: I understand that the machine is scouting, but how do we see what it finds?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: It's connected to my head. It's called a frequency.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Frequency?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: It's like an invisible signal. Think of it as similar to sound. It's called retinal vision. When the drone sends a signal to my frequency, an image appears on my retina. Look closely at my left eye.
An electric field-like image appeared in Kanya's pupil, quickly displaying an aerial view of the canyon.
Despite its small size, the resolution was high enough to see the unfolding scenery.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amazing technology.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Where can I get one of these?
Tess couldn't hide her envy.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: You don't get it; they're issued. Only the Mecha tribe receives these. At twelve, you get a drone.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: So you can't sneak one out?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No. They're strictly controlled. If caught, you'd face severe punishment.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: I see. I wish I had one...
Jealous, Tess fell silent.
I found a place where the Nor might be staying. It's not far from here.
Kanya started running.
She arrived at the cave entrance.
The drone landed on her hand, transforming into a gauntlet.
The final captured scene appeared on the surface.
Comparing it to the view, Kanya confirmed it was the right place and approached the cave.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Wait. I smell blood.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Blood? I don't smell anything.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Haha, of course not. I've been trained since childhood. Your machine can't smell?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: What? The Mecha tribe's technology can distinguish 140,000 scents. The drone just doesn't have that function.
Only then did the group realize Tess and the drone were having a subtle rivalry.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Enough. Just get on with it. Don't feel inferior to a machine.
Feeling better at Rian's words, Tess used her Sensory Schema to trace the scent of blood.
Taking a step into the cave, she scanned the interior and looked outside for a while.
After her analysis, she spoke to the group.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: There's definitely a scent of blood inside. Someone is in there, but it seems they haven't left the cave.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why? Isn't there any blood outside?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: No. The scent is stronger inside, suggesting they didn't leave.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But you checked outside for a long time?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: It's a kind of manual. If there was blood outside, the cave might be a trap.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So, it's not a trap. Let's go in.
Shirone cast the lighting spell Shining.
As the orb of light floated above his palm, Kanya and Rena were startled.
The Nor could use magic, but they had never seen such a spell in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: Sister, did you see that? He made light with his body.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: Yeah. The people from the land use strange magic.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: Yes. But doesn't that light... look like it?
Kanya looked at Rena in shock.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Rena! Such thoughts are blasphemy. Apologize in your heart right now.
Rena's expression turned sullen.
